chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: The next element we are going to study in HTML is my anchor element.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: Anchor element is used to add links to our page.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: That is, we go to many websites where we can click on any text and go to another page.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: Like if I take a normal Wikipedia example,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: let's suppose we go to the Avengers page of Wikipedia,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: so if we click on talk here, then what will happen is we will go to another page.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: Or if we click on this based on, then what will happen is we will go to another section or page.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: So the work of this method,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: means we go to another place by clicking on something,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: we use it in the form of anchor tag.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: What do we call this? We call it links.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: Or in HTML language, it is called anchor tag.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: So if we want to add links on our page, then we use anchor tags.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: Anchor tag means our basic A tag.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: If we simply write A,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: then it is called my anchor tag.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: But just writing anchor tag does not do anything.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: We have to link with anchor tag that where do you want to go by clicking on it.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: Like if we want to go to google.com,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: so to link, the opening tag of anchor tag,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: this is my opening tag, this is my closing tag,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: in the opening tag, we have our href, this is my attribute,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: what do we call this? We call this attribute.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: So the full form of href is,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: we have hypertext reference.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: Now this sounds heavy, but its basic meaning is link.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: Which link do you want to go by clicking on it.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: So the value of hyperlink reference,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: we will set it as google.com link.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: So we write the whole link, not just google.com,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: we have to write the link of the whole website.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: And then we can write any text in the anchor tag,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: by clicking on the text, we will reach that link.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: Now all these things that we have said,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: we will do them slowly.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: What we will do is, we will remove the old unordered list code,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: save and refresh.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: Here we will do tags in html, refresh.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: And now what we will do is, we will create an anchor tag.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: To create an anchor tag, we will simply write a.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: We will save and refresh.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: So simply writing a will not do anything on the screen.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: But when we will write some text inside this anchor tag,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: then that text will become clickable.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: Now if I write google, save and refresh.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: So now what happens is,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: only the normal google is displayed here.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: But if we add google link,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: we will add the link in the opening tag,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: we will write href,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: hyper reference is equal to double quotes,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: this is my attribute.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: And what we will write inside the attribute,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: let's suppose we wrote google.com,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: as soon as we click on google,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: we reach google.com, save and refresh.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: What is google.com? It is a link.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: So what will happen is,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: it will start giving us a message.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: And as soon as we click on it,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: we will reach this page.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: Here it is saying,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: your file could not be accessed.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: It is saying, maybe your file has been moved,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: edited or deleted.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: Error is showing file not found.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: So what did it do?

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: HTML understood that in href,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: the google.com you have written,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: you are trying to say that

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: google.com is a file of your computer,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: which is now trying to find the browser.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: But google.com is not a file,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: google.com is a complete website.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: But on that website,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: as soon as you type google.com,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: the link of that website is not only google.com,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: as soon as we click on it,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: and expand it,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: what happens after clicking?

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: As I clicked here,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: we will click again,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: by clicking twice, it will expand.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: So we don't just search on google.com,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: whenever we write google.com in our browser,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: we type https,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: colon, double slash,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: www.google.com,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: this is the complete link of google.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: And we have to copy and paste this link.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: What is https,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: what is www,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: these are the full forms,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: which we have learnt in our childhood.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: Https is our protocol,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: which we will learn later.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: WWW means World Wide Web.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: So google.com will be searched on the whole web.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: Basically, if we want to link with google.com,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: we have to write the complete link.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: We will click on save,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: and refresh it.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: Now if we refresh,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: there will be no change in the look.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: But as soon as we click on it,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: we will reach google.com.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: We can back it.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: In the same way,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: we can create another anchor tag.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: We will write netflix in this anchor tag.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: And we will link it with netflix.com.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: I have written href,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: and we will take the complete link of netflix.com.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: netflix.com

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: We will copy and paste it.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: So netflix.com is https,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: and www.netflix.com

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: and slash IN.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: Slash IN usually means India in the links.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: If there is a website,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: which has to be shown in a different version for US,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: it is written as US.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: If it is a different version for UK,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: then it will be slash UK or something like this.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: Like here,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: we are just guessing,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: that instead of IN, if we write UK and refresh it,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: it is still IN.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: By default, it is tracking that our IP is India.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: So we will cross this.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: And now what we will do is,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: we have written netflix here,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: and we will link it with netflix.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: So one link is of google,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: and the other is of netflix.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: Now if we click on google,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: it will reach google.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: If we click on netflix,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: it will reach netflix.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: In this way,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: there are multiple websites

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: that we can link with.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: Now in our anchor tag,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: which is my href,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: we can add two types of links.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: One link is absolute link,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: and the other is relative link.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: Absolute link means,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: the link that is directly visible on the internet,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: and has been pasted there.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: In absolute link,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: like we have linked google,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: and netflix,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: these types of links are called absolute links.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: Relative links are

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: related links.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: In relative links,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: we usually write file links.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: File links means,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: if we are on hello world.html,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: then usually when we make big projects,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: to go from one page to another page,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: from one html page to another html page,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: we write like this.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: Let's say I have made the option of home.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: And to link home

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: with another page,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: we will use our relative link.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: So this other page,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: this will also be a file

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: that is in our system.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: Relative means,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: the file we are working on,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: we are writing our link in its relative.

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: So usually,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: if we have made another index.html file,

chunks.title: Anchor Element
chunks.text: then we can also write

chunks.title: Anchor Element

text: The next element we are going to study in HTML is my anchor element. Anchor element is used to add links to our page. That is, we go to many websites where we can click on any text and go to another page. Like if I take a normal Wikipedia example, let's suppose we go to the Avengers page of Wikipedia, so if we click on talk here, then what will happen is we will go to another page. Or if we click on this based on, then what will happen is we will go to another section or page. So the work of this method, means we go to another place by clicking on something, we use it in the form of anchor tag. What do we call this? We call it links. Or in HTML language, it is called anchor tag. So if we want to add links on our page, then we use anchor tags. Anchor tag means our basic A tag. If we simply write A, then it is called my anchor tag. But just writing anchor tag does not do anything. We have to link with anchor tag that where do you want to go by clicking on it. Like if we want to go to google.com, so to link, the opening tag of anchor tag, this is my opening tag, this is my closing tag, in the opening tag, we have our href, this is my attribute, what do we call this? We call this attribute. So the full form of href is, we have hypertext reference. Now this sounds heavy, but its basic meaning is link. Which link do you want to go by clicking on it. So the value of hyperlink reference, we will set it as google.com link. So we write the whole link, not just google.com, we have to write the link of the whole website. And then we can write any text in the anchor tag, by clicking on the text, we will reach that link. Now all these things that we have said, we will do them slowly. What we will do is, we will remove the old unordered list code, save and refresh. Here we will do tags in html, refresh. And now what we will do is, we will create an anchor tag. To create an anchor tag, we will simply write a. We will save and refresh. So simply writing a will not do anything on the screen. But when we will write some text inside this anchor tag, then that text will become clickable. Now if I write google, save and refresh. So now what happens is, only the normal google is displayed here. But if we add google link, we will add the link in the opening tag, we will write href, hyper reference is equal to double quotes, this is my attribute. And what we will write inside the attribute, let's suppose we wrote google.com, as soon as we click on google, we reach google.com, save and refresh. What is google.com? It is a link. So what will happen is, it will start giving us a message. And as soon as we click on it, we will reach this page. Here it is saying, your file could not be accessed. It is saying, maybe your file has been moved, edited or deleted. Error is showing file not found. So what did it do? HTML understood that in href, the google.com you have written, you are trying to say that google.com is a file of your computer, which is now trying to find the browser. But google.com is not a file, google.com is a complete website. But on that website, as soon as you type google.com, the link of that website is not only google.com, as soon as we click on it, and expand it, what happens after clicking? As I clicked here, we will click again, by clicking twice, it will expand. So we don't just search on google.com, whenever we write google.com in our browser, we type https, colon, double slash, www.google.com, this is the complete link of google. And we have to copy and paste this link. What is https, what is www, these are the full forms, which we have learnt in our childhood. Https is our protocol, which we will learn later. WWW means World Wide Web. So google.com will be searched on the whole web. Basically, if we want to link with google.com, we have to write the complete link. We will click on save, and refresh it. Now if we refresh, there will be no change in the look. But as soon as we click on it, we will reach google.com. We can back it. In the same way, we can create another anchor tag. We will write netflix in this anchor tag. And we will link it with netflix.com. I have written href, and we will take the complete link of netflix.com. netflix.com We will copy and paste it. So netflix.com is https, and www.netflix.com and slash IN. Slash IN usually means India in the links. If there is a website, which has to be shown in a different version for US, it is written as US. If it is a different version for UK, then it will be slash UK or something like this. Like here, we are just guessing, that instead of IN, if we write UK and refresh it, it is still IN. By default, it is tracking that our IP is India. So we will cross this. And now what we will do is, we have written netflix here, and we will link it with netflix. So one link is of google, and the other is of netflix. Now if we click on google, it will reach google. If we click on netflix, it will reach netflix. In this way, there are multiple websites that we can link with. Now in our anchor tag, which is my href, we can add two types of links. One link is absolute link, and the other is relative link. Absolute link means, the link that is directly visible on the internet, and has been pasted there. In absolute link, like we have linked google, and netflix, these types of links are called absolute links. Relative links are related links. In relative links, we usually write file links. File links means, if we are on hello world.html, then usually when we make big projects, to go from one page to another page, from one html page to another html page, we write like this. Let's say I have made the option of home. And to link home with another page, we will use our relative link. So this other page, this will also be a file that is in our system. Relative means, the file we are working on, we are writing our link in its relative. So usually, if we have made another index.html file, then we can also write its link in href. But for now, we are not doing that. Because for now, we have started learning new html. For now, we will learn to link our projects later. We will learn to link multiple pages. But for now, absolute links are those links that connect us to something hosted on the internet. And in relative links, we are talking about those links that exist somewhere in our file system.
